authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
USDA Forest Service — Tahoe National Forest / LTBMU (Granite Chief Wilderness)

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Five Lakes not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Five Lakes not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 07/10/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW (no entry found for Five Lakes)
Granite Chief Wilderness — Recreation Site, Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit, USDA Forest Service
sources.quotedText: Wilderness permits are not presently required for day or overnight use in the Granite Chief Wilderness. Camping, campfires, and stove use are prohibited within 600 feet of any lake in the Five Lakes basin. Group size is limited to 12 people for day and overnight use.

sources.title: Five Lakes Trail — Truckee Trails Foundation
sources.quotedText: Camping is not allowed in the 5 Lakes Basin and please refrain from campfires during peak fire season.

sources.title: Granite Chief Wilderness — primitive and natural beauty | Fly Fishing the Sierra
sources.quotedText: The lakes were stocked with both Brookies and Rainbows up until the mid 1990's... The largest lake still contains a nice population of Rainbows. Brookies in the other smaller lakes. Five Lakes form the headwaters of Five Lakes creek which flows into the Rubicon and Hells Hole Reservoir.

sources.title: Five Lakes Trailhead (in Placer County, CA) — HomeTownLocator
USGS National Hydrography Dataset (NHD) Waterbody layer — 5 lake polygons sharing GNIS_ID 00223566, "Five Lakes", Placer Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"GNIS_ID":"00223566","GNIS_NAME":"Five Lakes","AREASQKM":0.015,"FTYPE":390,"PERMANENT_IDENTIFIER":"61231591"} / {..."AREASQKM":0.007,..."PERMANENT_IDENTIFIER":"61231553"} / {..."AREASQKM":0.006,..."PERMANENT_IDENTIFIER":"61231555"} / {..."AREASQKM":0.027,..."PERMANENT_IDENTIFIER":"61223949"} / {..."AREASQKM":0.012,..."PERMANENT_IDENTIFIER":"61231589"}. All 5 polygons carry GNIS_ID 00223566 / GNIS_NAME 'Five Lakes'; centroid lon/lat used here (-120.2502, 39.1759) is the simple mean of the 5 polygon centroids (39.17468/-120.25032, 39.17761/-120.24998, 39.17623/-120.25032, 39.17491/-120.25264, 39.17615/-120.24763). Replaces the file's prior lon/lat (-120.245, 39.17), which had no cited GNIS/NHD source and sits ~795 m from this GNIS-linked centroid; the geonames.MapServer point gazetteer (layer 7) has no separate point record for gaz_id 223566 in Placer Co. — a distinct, unrelated 'Five Lakes Basin' (gaz_id 260178) exists ~38 km northwest in Nevada Co. near English Mountain and must not be confused with this one.

parameters.note: Five Lakes is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Five Lakes sits within the Granite Chief Wilderness (Tahoe National Forest / Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit). No wilderness permit is currently required for day or overnight use, but camping, campfires, and stove use are prohibited within 600 feet of any lake in the Five Lakes basin, and group size is capped at 12 for day and overnight use.

regulations.citation: USFS Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit — Granite Chief Wilderness recreation page
regulations.humanSummary: No wilderness permit required, but camping/campfires/stoves are banned within 600 ft of any Five Lakes basin lake; group size limited to 12
regulations.verbatimText: Wilderness permits are not presently required for day or overnight use in the Granite Chief Wilderness. Camping, campfires, and stove use are prohibited within 600 feet of any lake in the Five Lakes basin. Group size is limited to 12 people for day and overnight use.
